title: Single Cell RNA Sequencing (scRNA-seq)
author: Edgar Otto
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7dthi6n
materials: Place the Chip in the Holder before loading reagents
Gently pipette mix with Wide Bore pipette tip
After loading Master Mix, wait for 00:00:30
Then, immediately proceed to loading the Gel Beads
Fold and lock Chip Holder at 45° angle
Cycle in Bio-Rad C1000 TouchTM thermal cycler with Deep Well Module

steps.title: Remove biopsy frozen in 500 µL...
steps.instruction: Remove biopsy frozen in 500 µL CryoStor solution in a 1.8 mL cryovial out of the liquid nitrogen tank . Thaw frozen biopsy in a 37 °C water bath under constant swirling for 60-90 sec only until biopsy at the bottom is ice-free.

steps.title: Transfer biopsy to plastic petri dish (ø 3
steps.instruction: Transfer biopsy to plastic petri dish (ø 3.5 cm) filled with 1 ml DMEM/F12/10%FBS for 00:00:10 to wash off remaining DMSO at room temperature.

steps.title: Transfer to second plastic petri dish (ø 3
steps.instruction: Transfer to second plastic petri dish (ø 3.5 cm) filled with 1 ml DMEM/F12/10%FBS and incubate for 00:10:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Take picture of biopsy together with a ruler
steps.instruction: Take picture of biopsy together with a ruler.

steps.title: Cut biopsy in ~1 mm long pieces using a scalpel
steps.instruction: Cut biopsy in ~1 mm long pieces using a scalpel.

steps.title: Prepare dissociation medium
steps.instruction: Prepare dissociation medium: 450 µL DMEM/F12 (no FBS) and 50 µL µl Liberase TL (conc.: 2.5 mg/ml H 2 O, final enzyme conc.: 0.25 mg/ml in 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube (noBind).

steps.title: Transfer biopsy tissue to prepared dissociation...
steps.instruction: Transfer biopsy tissue to prepared dissociation medium prewarmed to 37 °C .

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:12:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C for 00:12:00 and shake at 500 rpm in an Eppendorf Thermomixer.

steps.title: Triturate after 00:06:00 for 15 times...
steps.instruction: Triturate after 00:06:00 for 15 times with wide bore 1 mL pipette tip.

steps.title: Stop reaction with 500 µL DMEM/F12/10%FBS...
steps.instruction: Stop reaction with 500 µL DMEM/F12/10%FBS (room temperature) and gently mix.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:01:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:01:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Filter through 30 µm gaze (Miltenyi)...
steps.instruction: Filter through 30 µm gaze (Miltenyi) into 15 mL tube on ice.

steps.title: Push tissue through gaze using a...
steps.instruction: Push tissue through gaze using a 5 mL syringe plunger (rubber side).

steps.title: Wash filter with 10 mL cold DMEM/F12/10%FBS
steps.instruction: Wash filter with 10 mL cold DMEM/F12/10%FBS.

steps.title: Filter single cell suspension again through...
steps.instruction: Filter single cell suspension again through new 30 µm gaze (Miltenyi) into a second 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Wash first 15 mL tube with...
steps.instruction: Wash first 15 mL tube with 1 mL cold DMEM/F12/10% FBS and apply through 30 µm gaze into second 15 mL tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge cell suspension at 200 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cell suspension at 200 x g for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Remove supernatant using glass Pasteur pipette and house vacuum
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant using glass Pasteur pipette and house vacuum. (~ 20 µL remain)

steps.title: Add and dispense cell pellet carefully with 40 µL DMEM/F12/10%FBS
steps.instruction: Add and dispense cell pellet carefully with 40 µL DMEM/F12/10%FBS.

steps.title: Take 5 µL for cell count...
steps.instruction: Take 5 µL for cell count and mix with 5 µL Trypan blue.

steps.title: Count viable cells on hemocytometer or...
steps.instruction: Count viable cells on hemocytometer or with automated cell counter (Countess)

steps.title: Load up to 10,000 viable cells...
steps.instruction: Load up to 10,000 viable cells in up to 46 µL of the cell suspension to 10X Chromium array ASAP.

steps.title: Place the 1
steps.instruction: Place the 1.8 mL cryovial with 1.5 mL HypoThermosol on ice

steps.title: Add the renal biopsy specimen to...
steps.instruction: Add the renal biopsy specimen to the cryovial with HypoThermosol immediately.

steps.title: Keep upright and at 4 °C...
steps.instruction: Keep upright and at 4 °C (on ice) between the collection step and the cryopreservation step.

steps.title: Place the 1
steps.instruction: Place the 1.8 mL cryovial with 1.5 mL CryoStor on ice.

steps.title: Transfer the renal biopsy specimen to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the renal biopsy specimen to the cryovial with CryoStor CS10 very quickly.

steps.title: Keep the cryovial upright and on...
steps.instruction: Keep the cryovial upright and on ice for 00:15:00 to allow permeabilization.

steps.title: Place the cryovial in a Mr
steps.instruction: Place the cryovial in a Mr. Frosty freezing unit containing isopropanol.

steps.title: Place the Mr
steps.instruction: Place the Mr. Frosty container in the -80 °C freezer for 24:00:00 .

steps.title: Transfer the cryovial from the Mr
steps.instruction: Transfer the cryovial from the Mr. Frosty container to liquid N2, submerged. (Cryovials can be shipped on dry ice)

steps.title: Place the 1
steps.instruction: Place the 1.8 mL cryovial with 1.5 mL HypoThermosol on ice

steps.title: Add the renal biopsy specimen to...
steps.instruction: Add the renal biopsy specimen to the cryovial with HypoThermosol immediately.

steps.title: Keep upright and at 4 °C...
steps.instruction: Keep upright and at 4 °C (on ice) between the collection step and the cryopreservation step.

steps.title: Place the 1
steps.instruction: Place the 1.8 mL cryovial with 1.5 mL CryoStor on ice.

steps.title: Transfer the renal biopsy specimen to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the renal biopsy specimen to the cryovial with CryoStor CS10 very quickly.

steps.title: Keep the cryovial upright and on...
steps.instruction: Keep the cryovial upright and on ice for 00:15:00 to allow permeabilization.

steps.title: Place the cryovial in a Mr
steps.instruction: Place the cryovial in a Mr. Frosty freezing unit containing isopropanol.

steps.title: Place the Mr
steps.instruction: Place the Mr. Frosty container in the -80°C freezer for 24:00:00 .

steps.title: Transfer the cryovial from the Mr
steps.instruction: Transfer the cryovial from the Mr. Frosty container to liquid N2, submerged. (Cryovials can be shipped on dry ice)

steps.title: A fully dissociated, single-cell suspension is...
steps.instruction: A fully dissociated, single-cell suspension is essential for the analysis of single-cell transcriptomes. a)Suspension cell lines, bead-enriched and flow-sorted cells can be used directly after washing. b)Adherent cell lines require trypsin treatment first. c)Single-cell suspension from tissues requires optimization of dissociation.

steps.title: It is important to treat cells...
steps.instruction: It is important to treat cells gently to minimize cell lysis and loss: a)When cells lyse, the mRNA will contaminate other GEMs. b)Wash and resuspend in PBS + 0.04% non-acetylated BSA to minimize cell loss during handling. c)Wash cells 2x using a wide-bore pipette tip to remove ambient RNA and contaminants.

steps.title: Use a cell strainer to remove...
steps.instruction: Use a cell strainer to remove clumps or debris from washed cells. a)Cell debris and fibers can be counted as either dead or live cells, causing inaccurate counts. b)Cell debris and large clumps may clog or wet the chip.

steps.title: Quantitate cells accurately before loading the system
steps.instruction: Quantitate cells accurately before loading the system. a)Approximately 65% loaded cells will be recovered. b)Recommended range: 500 to 10,000 recovered cells. c)Under- or over-loading may impact application performance.

steps.title: Store cells suspension on ice until...
steps.instruction: Store cells suspension on ice until you are ready to load the chip. a)Prepare cell suspension right before you are going to use it. b)Load cells as soon as possible after preparation, ideally within 30 min

steps.title: A fully dissociated, single cell suspension...
steps.instruction: A fully dissociated, single cell suspension is essential for the analysis of single cell transcriptomes. a)Suspension cell lines, bead-enriched and flow-sorted cells can be used directly after washing. b)Adherent cell lines require trypsin treatment first. c)Single cell suspension from tissues requires optimization of dissociation.

steps.title: It is important to treat cells...
steps.instruction: It is important to treat cells gently to minimize cell lysis and loss: a)When cells lyse, the mRNA will contaminate other GEMs. b)Wash and re-suspend in PBS + 0.04% non-acetylated BSA to minimize cell loss during handling. c)Wash cells 2x using a wide-bore pipette tip to remove ambient RNA and contaminants.

steps.title: Use a cell strainer to remove...
steps.instruction: Use a cell strainer to remove clumps or debris from washed cells. a)Cell debris and fibers can be counted as either dead or live cells, causing inaccurate counts. b)Cell debris and large clumps may clog or wet the chip.

steps.title: Quantitate cells accurately before loading the system
steps.instruction: Quantitate cells accurately before loading the system. a)Approximately 65% loaded cells will be recovered. b)Recommended range: 500 to 10,000 recovered cells. c)Under- or over-loading may impact application performance.

steps.title: Store cells suspension on ice until...
steps.instruction: Store cells suspension on ice until you are ready to load the chip. a)Prepare cell suspension right before you are going to use it. b)Load cells as soon as possible after preparation, ideally within 00:30:00

steps.title: Transfer cells in media to a 2 mL Eppendorf tube
steps.instruction: Transfer cells in media to a 2 mL Eppendorf tube

steps.title: Spin down cells to form pellet...
steps.instruction: Spin down cells to form pellet •Depending on cell size and concentration, pellet size varies

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant

steps.title: Gently add 1x PBS + 0
steps.instruction: Gently add 1x PBS + 0.04% BSA away from cell pellet

steps.title: Repeat the wash one more time
steps.instruction: Repeat the wash one more time

steps.title: Spin down cells to form pellet
steps.instruction: Spin down cells to form pellet

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 1x PBS + 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 1x PBS + 0.04% BSA with gentle pipette mix •For accurate cell counting, do not invert tubes

steps.title: Adjust to desired cell concentration as indicated in section 3
steps.instruction: Adjust to desired cell concentration as indicated in section 3.

steps.title: Equilibrate Single Cell 3’ Gel Beads...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate Single Cell 3’ Gel Beads to room temperature for at least 00:30:00 , thaw reagents

steps.title: Resuspend the RT primer in 40...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the RT primer in 40 µL l low TE buffer and prepare the thermal cycler.

steps.title: Prepare Master Mix on ice
steps.instruction: Prepare Master Mix on ice. Do NOT add cells yet.

steps.title: Handle the chip by its edges,...
steps.instruction: Handle the chip by its edges, taking care to avoid contacting the bottom surface (this helps to mitigate frictional charging)

steps.title: Align the beveled corners at upper...
steps.instruction: Align the beveled corners at upper left and insert chip under the guide at the left edge of the recess in the holder

steps.title: Press down on the right side...
steps.instruction: Press down on the right side of the chip until the spring loaded clip engages

steps.title: Close the Holder and lay flat on benchtop
steps.instruction: Close the Holder and lay flat on benchtop

steps.title: Dispense the 66
steps.instruction: Dispense the 66.2 µL of Master Mix into each well of an 8-tube strip on a chilled metal block resting on ice. Then add the calculated volume of water into each strip tube.

steps.title: The next step is critical to...
steps.instruction: The next step is critical to recovering the maximum number of cells. It is important to ensure that: a)all of the cells in the sample are suspended b)the sample is homogenous when adding the cell suspension volume to the Master Mix.

steps.title: Estimate the volume of the cell...
steps.instruction: Estimate the volume of the cell suspension and set the pipette at half that volume for pipette mixing. Wide bore pipette tips are not necessary for this step

steps.title: Pipette the required volume of cell...
steps.instruction: Pipette the required volume of cell suspension from the center of the suspension volume.

steps.title: Add the appropriate volume (µl) of...
steps.instruction: Add the appropriate volume (µl) of single cell suspension to each well of the tube strip containing the Master Mix.

steps.title: With a pipette set to 90...
steps.instruction: With a pipette set to 90 µL gently pipette mix the combined cells and Master Mix 5 times while keeping the tube strip on a chilled metal block resting on ice.

steps.title: Without discarding the pipette tips, transfer...
steps.instruction: Without discarding the pipette tips, transfer 90 µL Master Mix containing cells to the wells in the row labeled 1, taking care not to introduce bubbles.

steps.title: This wait time is required for...
steps.instruction: This wait time is required for correct priming of channels inside the Single Cell 3’ Chip

steps.title: Snap the Single Cell 3’ Gel...
steps.instruction: Snap the Single Cell 3’ Gel Bead Strip into a 10x Vortex Adapter and vortex for 00:00:30 . a)A 30 sec wait while vortexing the Single Cell 3’ Gel Bead Strip is required to ensure proper priming of the Master Mix containing cells in the Single Cell 3’ Chip.

steps.title: Remove the Single Cell 3’ Gel...
steps.instruction: Remove the Single Cell 3’ Gel Bead Strip and flick in a sharp, downward motion to ensure maximum recovery. Confirm that there are no bubbles at the bottom of the tube and that liquid levels are uniform.

steps.title: Pipette 40 µL l Gel Beads from Gel Bead Strip
steps.instruction: Pipette 40 µL l Gel Beads from Gel Bead Strip

steps.title: Dispense Gel Beads in row labeled...
steps.instruction: Dispense Gel Beads in row labeled 2 a)Dispense into bottom of well b)Slowly pipette Gel Beads c)Avoid air bubbles

steps.title: Transfer Partitioning Oil to a reservoir...
steps.instruction: Transfer Partitioning Oil to a reservoir and load row labeled 3 a)Once oil is added, run the chip within 00:02:00 . The less time the chip sits, the better. b)NEVER forget to add full volume of oil – doing so can damage the Chromium™ Controller! c)Don’t leave oil in reagent reservoir for too long – avoid evaporation and contamination by particulates

steps.title: Attach the 10x™ Gasket
steps.instruction: Attach the 10x™ Gasket. The notched cut should be at the top left corner. a)Ensure the gasket holes are aligned with the chip wells. b)Do not touch or “smooth” the top surface of the gasket. c)Keep the assembly horizontal to avoid wetting the 10x Gasket with Partitioning Oil.

steps.title: Load assembled Chip, Holder and Gasket
steps.instruction: Load assembled Chip, Holder and Gasket.

steps.title: Keep the Chip Holder horizontal during...
steps.instruction: Keep the Chip Holder horizontal during transfer to the instrument to avoid wetting the gasket with reagents.

steps.title: Confirm Chromium Single Cell A program...
steps.instruction: Confirm Chromium Single Cell A program and press play button to start run.

steps.title: The Controller will chime at the...
steps.instruction: The Controller will chime at the completion of the run (~ 00:06:30 ).

steps.title: Maintain an Eppendorf twin
steps.instruction: Maintain an Eppendorf twin.tec® 96-Well PCR plate for GEM transfer on a chilled metal block resting on ice.

steps.title: Remove the Single Cell 3’ Chip from the Chromium Controller
steps.instruction: Remove the Single Cell 3’ Chip from the Chromium Controller.

steps.title: Open the 10x Chip Holder and...
steps.instruction: Open the 10x Chip Holder and fold the lid back until it clicks to expose the wells at a 45-degree angle.

steps.title: Check for volume uniformity in the
steps.instruction: Check for volume uniformity in the: a)Sample (1) b)Gel Bead (2) and c)Partitioning Oil (3) wells remaining in the Single Cell 3’ Chip.

steps.title: Remove Gasket
steps.instruction: Remove Gasket

steps.title: Transfer 100 µL GEMs to twinc
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 µL GEMs to twinc.tec® PCR plate a)Aspirate GEMs •Within 00:02:00 of completed run •Pipette tip just above lowest point in recovery well •Do not take more than 100 µl or double-dip b)Dispense into PCR Plate •Pipette tip against side of well •Dispense slowly (20 sec) to avoid air bubbles •Keep plate on chilled block

steps.title: Withdraw pipette tips from the wells...
steps.instruction: Withdraw pipette tips from the wells and verify that there is no air in the tips. GEMs should appear opaque and uniform across all channels.

steps.title: Seal plate with Bio-Rad PX1TM Plate...
steps.instruction: Seal plate with Bio-Rad PX1TM Plate Sealer a)Red stripe facing up b)A tight seal is critical to GEM stability

steps.title: Store in the PCR plate at...
steps.instruction: Store in the PCR plate at 4 °C for up to 72:00:00 before proceeding to Post GEM-RT Cleanup.

steps.title: Add 125 µL of Recovery Agent...
steps.instruction: Add 125 µL of Recovery Agent directly to samples in PCR plate.

steps.title: With the same tips, move entire...
steps.instruction: With the same tips, move entire volume to 8 tube strips. (If PCR strip tubes have been used for GEM-RT reaction, no volume transfer into a new 8 tube strip is necessary) a)Make sure to extract everything from the 96 well plate b)Do not vortex or pipette mix after adding Recovery Agent

steps.title: Slowly remove 125 µL Recovery Agent/Partitioning...
steps.instruction: Slowly remove 125 µL Recovery Agent/Partitioning Oil (pink) from the bottom of the tubes and discard a)Some Recovery Agent may remain b)Be careful to not aspirate any aqueous phase

steps.title: Set up cDNA Amplification Master Mix...
steps.instruction: Set up cDNA Amplification Master Mix a)The optimal number of cycles for the cDNA amplification reaction is a trade-off between generating sufficient mass and minimizing PCR amplification artifacts

steps.title: Bioanalyzer QC
steps.instruction: Bioanalyzer QC. Optimal performance has been characterized on input cDNA with a mean length > 2 kb.

steps.title: The library mRNA prep is sequenced...
steps.instruction: The library mRNA prep is sequenced on an Illumina HiSeq 4000 platform. The output from the sequencer is then analyzed using the 10XGenomics software CellRanger. This step includes the alignment of the reads to human genome (GRCH38). The raw file output from CellRanger is further processed using Seurat R package. The downstream processing includes: Log transformation, filtering out cells with < 500 genes (or < 400 UMI) and > 5000 genes, filtering out cells with mitochondrial content > 20%, scaling based on UMI count, mitochondrial read content, principal component analysis, unsupervised clustering based on significant pca components, tSNEplot visualization and differential expression analysis using Wilcox test. The library mRNA prep is sequenced on an Illumina HiSeq 4000 platform. The output from the sequencer is then analyzed using the 10XGenomics software CellRanger. This step includes the alignment of the reads to human genome (GRCH38). The raw file output from CellRanger is further processed using Seurat R package. The downstream processing includes: Log transformation, filtering out cells with < 500 genes (or < 400 UMI) and > 5000 genes, filtering out cells with mitochondrial content > 20%, scaling based on UMI count, mitochondrial read content, principal component analysis, unsupervised clustering based on significant pca components, tSNEplot visualization and differential expression analysis using Wilcox test.
